hook: Tension in attraction is usually better created through timing than through edginess.
body: Pauses, eye contact, selective disclosure, and playful contrast often create more spark than trying to act provocative.

hook: A lot of bad flirting comes from forcing polarity before trust or rhythm exist.
body: Create charge by not overexplaining and by letting moments breathe long enough to register.

body: Holding a look for one second longer and smiling can do more than another layer of verbal escalation.

body: Experiment with restraint first: say a little less, rush a little less, and notice what the silence does.
